skill.name: computer-use-agents
skill.description: Build AI agents that interact with computers like humans do - viewing screens, moving cursors, clicking buttons, and typing text. Covers Anthropic's Computer Use, OpenAI's Operator/CUA, and open-source implementations.
skill.summary: Learn patterns and implementations for building AI agents that control computers through vision and automation.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. This skill contains educational documentation and code examples for building computer-use agents. The detected patterns (external commands, filesystem access, screen capture) are legitimate code examples demonstrating automation tools like pyautogui and subprocess in Docker-sandboxed environments. The skill explicitly teaches security best practices including sandboxing, resource limits, and credential isolation. No malicious intent or executable code present.

low_findings.title: External Commands in Documentation
low_findings.description: Code examples show pyautogui and subprocess usage for computer automation. These are legitimate educational examples demonstrating how to build computer-use agents. The skill includes extensive security warnings and sandboxing guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Clear educational context - code examples in documentation showing automation patterns, not executable code

low_findings.title: Filesystem Access in Examples
low_findings.description: Code examples reference /tmp/screenshot.png for temporary file storage. This is standard temp directory usage in demo code, not suspicious filesystem access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard temp file usage in educational example - no exfiltration or modification of sensitive files

low_findings.title: Screen Capture References
low_findings.description: Documentation mentions screenshot capabilities as a core feature of computer-use agents. This is documented functionality, not malicious surveillance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Screen capture is the primary purpose of computer-use agents - clearly documented feature, not hidden malware

content.user_title: Build AI agents that control computers
content.value_statement: Manual desktop automation is repetitive and time-consuming. This skill teaches you to build AI agents that observe screens, reason about tasks, and execute actions autonomously using computer use patterns.
actual_capabilities: Explain perception-reasoning-action loop architecture for computer use agents
Provide code examples for screen capture and analysis using vision models
Demonstrate Docker sandboxing patterns for safe agent deployment
Show implementation of Anthropic Computer Use API with tool definitions
Teach mouse/keyboard automation using pyautogui and subprocess
Guide resource limits and security constraints for agent isolation
limitations: Provides educational documentation and code examples only, not pre-built executable agents
Requires users to set up their own Docker environment and API credentials
Focuses on Python-based implementations using Anthropic and open-source tools
Screen automation may struggle with complex UI elements like dropdowns and scrollbars

use_cases.title: QA Engineer Automating Browser Tests
use_cases.description: Build an agent that navigates web applications, verifies UI elements, and captures screenshots of bugs. The agent clicks through user flows, checks for expected content, and reports visual regressions automatically.

use_cases.title: Data Entry Automation for Business Operations
use_cases.description: Create an agent that transfers data between legacy desktop applications without APIs. The agent reads information from spreadsheets, opens target applications, and inputs data through keyboard and mouse actions.

use_cases.title: Accessibility Testing Assistant
use_cases.description: Develop an agent that explores web pages to identify accessibility issues. It tests keyboard navigation, screen reader compatibility, and color contrast by interacting with pages like a human user would.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Computer Use Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to build a simple AI agent that can take screenshots and click on buttons. Show me a minimal Python example using pyautogui and explain the perception-reasoning-action loop step by step.

prompt_templates.title: Docker Sandbox Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a secure Docker container for running computer use agents. I need Xvfb for virtual display, VNC for observation, and proper resource limits. Generate a Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml with security best practices.

prompt_templates.title: Anthropic Computer Use Integration
prompt_templates.prompt: I have an Anthropic API key. Show me how to implement a complete computer use agent using the official Anthropic SDK with bash tools and text editor capabilities. Include error handling and step limits.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Step Workflow Automation
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an agent that can automate a multi-step workflow: open a browser, navigate to a URL, log in with credentials from environment variables, download a report, and save it to a specific directory. Include context management to track state across steps.

output_examples.input: Show me how to capture a screenshot in a computer use agent
output_examples.output: The code example demonstrates capturing screenshots using pyautogui.screenshot(), resizing to 1280x800 for token efficiency, encoding to base64, and returning the image data to send to a vision model for analysis.

output_examples.input: How do I safely run a computer use agent?
output_examples.output: The skill provides a complete Docker setup with a non-root user, read-only filesystem, resource limits (2 CPU, 4GB RAM), internal networking, and seccomp profiles. The agent runs in /tmp with no access to host credentials or sensitive directories.

best_practices: Always run computer use agents in isolated Docker containers with resource limits and no access to host credentials
Implement step limits (max 50 iterations) and timeouts to prevent runaway agent loops
Use keyboard shortcuts instead of mouse clicks when possible for more reliable automation
anti_patterns: Never run computer use agents on your host machine with access to real files and credentials
Avoid hardcoded credentials - always use environment variables or secret management tools
Do not skip the sandboxing step even for testing - agents can execute unintended actions

faq.question: What AI models work best for computer use agents?
faq.answer: Claude Opus 4.5 and Claude Sonnet 4 are currently the best models for computer use tasks. They have native computer use capabilities and understand UI elements better than general vision models.

faq.question: Can I use this skill to automate any desktop application?
faq.answer: While computer use agents can interact with most desktop applications, some UI elements like dropdowns, scrollbars, and canvas-based interfaces may be challenging. The skill teaches patterns that work for standard UI controls.

faq.question: How do I prevent the agent from damaging my system?
faq.answer: Always use Docker sandboxing as shown in the examples. Restrict network access, use read-only filesystems, run as non-root, and never provide access to sensitive directories or credentials.

faq.question: What is the perception-reasoning-action loop?
faq.answer: It is the core pattern: capture screen (perception), send to vision model for analysis and planning (reasoning), execute mouse/keyboard action (action), then observe results and repeat until the task is complete.

faq.question: Can computer use agents work in headless environments?
faq.answer: Yes, using Xvfb (X Virtual Framebuffer) to create a virtual display. The skill shows how to configure Docker containers with Xvfb and VNC for remote observation of agent actions.

faq.question: How long does a typical agent task take?
faq.answer: Each perception-reasoning-action cycle takes 1-5 seconds for model inference plus action execution time. Simple tasks may take 10-30 seconds while complex workflows can take several minutes.
